NVDA.title: SA Asks: What's the most attractive robotics stock right now? (TER:NASDAQ)
NVDA.summary: Seeking Alpha analysts Nelson Alves and Michael Del Monte weigh in on the most attractive robotics stocks. Alves picks Teradyne (TER) due to its strong robotics portfolio and AI semiconductor testing business. Del Monte highlights Zebra Technologies (ZBRA) for its growth potential through partnerships and considers Symbotic (SYM) as an emerging opportunity.

NVDA.title: 1 Stock That's More Than Doubled in 3 Years, and 3 Reasons It Will Keep Soaring
NVDA.summary: General Motors (GM) stock has more than doubled in the last three years, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. This strong performance is attributed to GM's aggressive share buyback strategy, the high-margin recurring revenue generated by its OnStar and Super Cruise software services, and its pathway to electric vehicle (EV) profitability within three to five years. These factors suggest that GM is well-positioned for continued growth and could maintain its market-beating performance.

NVDA.title: Intel Corporation (INTC)’s Stock Has Surged but Needs an Engineering Revival
NVDA.summary: Intel Corporation's stock has surged over 550% in the past year due to new chip partnerships and government support, but the company still faces significant manufacturing challenges. Despite increased investor interest and analyst upgrades, its Intel Foundry segment reported a substantial operational loss in Q1. While Intel is making progress in manufacturing and benefiting from AI demand, some analysts suggest other AI stocks might offer better upside potential with less risk.

NVDA.title: 1 Unstoppable Trend That Could Supercharge Ford Stock by 2030
NVDA.summary: Ford is pivoting its manufacturing focus from Electric Vehicles (EVs) to producing battery energy storage systems (BESS) for AI data centers, which are facing significant power shortages. This strategic shift involves repurposing existing EV infrastructure and is expected to provide a new, stable revenue stream for Ford, potentially leading to a higher valuation for the company's stock. The company aims to manufacture 20 GWh of energy storage capacity annually, with the first BESS systems shipping as soon as 2027.

NVDA.title: Constellation Energy (CEG) and Walmart Announce Long-Term Nuclear Power Purchase Agreement
NVDA.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) and Walmart have signed a long-term agreement for emissions-free nuclear power from Constellation's Dresden Clean Energy Center in Illinois. This deal, involving 176 MW of wholesale supply and 30 MW of expanded capacity, facilitates Walmart's access to cleaner energy and supports local energy infrastructure, starting with two 15-year terms in 2029 and 2030. The new power will specifically support Walmart's upcoming high-tech perishable distribution center in Belvidere, Illinois.

NVDA.title: Aggregate net profit up last year
NVDA.summary: Taiwan's 5,000 largest companies achieved a record high in aggregate net profit last year, increasing over 11% to NT$5.77 trillion, driven by strong global demand for AI applications. Contract chipmaker TSMC led in both net profit (NT$1.72 trillion) and revenue (NT$3.78 trillion), followed by other major tech and financial firms. Despite this overall growth, 772 companies reported losses, and the average return on equity fell to a three-year low, indicating uneven corporate performance.

NVDA.title: This Tiny AI Stock Is Up By 680% in a Year. Is It A Buy?
NVDA.summary: Aehr Test Systems, a specialist in stress-testing semiconductors for AI chips, has seen its stock rally by 680% in the past year, with over half of that gain in 2026 alone. The company is gaining significant traction with hyperscalers, securing substantial orders that could dramatically increase revenue, despite a high price-to-sales ratio and volatility. While its EV-related revenue declined, its focus on AI and data centers positions it for potential growth, making it an interesting, albeit risky, prospect for investors.

NVDA.title: Penguin Solutions (PENG) Becomes NVIDIA AI Factory Specialized Partner
NVDA.summary: Penguin Solutions Inc. (NASDAQ: PENG) has been designated an NVIDIA AI Factory Specialized Partner, marking a significant milestone in its decade-long collaboration with NVIDIA. This specialized status validates Penguin's robust capabilities in designing, deploying, and operating full-stack AI factories at scale, crucial for meeting accelerating demand in competitive industries. The company's proprietary platform integrates innovative offerings like OriginAI and ClusterWareAI, streamlining AI adoption for various end users.

MSFT.title: Constellation Energy (CEG) and Walmart Announce Long-Term Nuclear Power Purchase Agreement
MSFT.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) and Walmart have signed a long-term agreement for emissions-free nuclear power from Constellation's Dresden Clean Energy Center in Illinois. The new power will specifically support Walmart's upcoming high-tech perishable distribution center in Belvidere, Illinois.

MSFT.title: Vanguard VT Challenges State Street NZAC in Global ETF Comparison
MSFT.summary: This article compares two global ETFs: Vanguard Total World Stock ETF (VT) and State Street SPDR MSCI ACWI Climate Paris Aligned ETF (NZAC). VT offers broad global exposure at a lower cost and wider diversification, while NZAC prioritizes climate-related risk mitigation with a slightly higher expense ratio but also a higher dividend yield. The choice between them depends on an investor's goals regarding diversification versus climate-focused investing.

MSFT.title: Oracle Eliminates 21,000 Jobs As Tech Giant Replaces 13% of Global Workforce With AI
MSFT.summary: Oracle (ORCL) recently cut 21,000 jobs, or 13% of its global workforce, over the past year, attributing these reductions to the adoption and deployment of AI technologies. Despite significant spending on severance and massive capital expenditure for AI infrastructure, the company reported strong Q4 revenue growth of 21% year-over-year, with cloud infrastructure revenue surging by 93%. This situation highlights a conflict between Oracle's booming business driven by AI demand and the substantial costs and workforce restructuring involved in funding this growth.

MSFT.title: JFrog (FROG) Is Up 5.9% After Bullish Analyst Views On AI And Cloud Security Tools
MSFT.summary: JFrog (FROG) saw a 5.9% stock increase following positive analyst commentary on its AI-focused tools and cloud security offerings. Analysts highlighted JFrog's role in managing AI development workflows and securing cloud-native pipelines as key drivers of demand, reinforcing AI and security as central catalysts. Despite this optimism, the article warns investors to consider competitive threats in the security market that could impact pricing power and valuation.

MSFT.title: Alphabet C background on Class C structure, shares shaped by long term strategy
MSFT.summary: This article explains the background and structure of Alphabet Inc.'s Class C shares (GOOG), highlighting their non-voting status while fully participating in the company's economic performance. It details how this dual-class structure, introduced in 2014, preserves founder control and is a common arrangement among large U.S. technology companies. The article also covers Alphabet's core businesses, governance model, and where Class C shares are traded.

MSFT.title: Wesbanco Bank Inc. Sells 35,099 Shares of Chevron Corporation $CVX
MSFT.summary: Wesbanco Bank Inc. reduced its stake in Chevron Corporation (NYSE:CVX) by 12.8% in the first quarter of 2026, selling 35,099 shares and retaining 239,956 shares valued at approximately $49.6 million. Despite this reduction, Chevron remains a significant holding for the firm, representing 1.4% of their portfolio. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Chevron with an average price target of $205.70, following recent positive news including its earnings beat and strategic deals with Microsoft and expansion into Greece.

MSFT.title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Is A Top Richard Chilton AI Stock
MSFT.summary: Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) is identified as one of the best stocks to buy according to billionaire Richard Chilton, despite its shares being down 22% year-to-date. The company is making significant investments in AI and supercomputing data centers and securing partnerships, such as with ICON plc for enterprise software and Chevron for power procurement. While the company faces concerns over cloud infrastructure investment and slower legacy software growth, its long-term thesis is supported by strong network effects and an embedded ecosystem.

GOOGL.title: Autonomous & Self Driving Vehicle News: Waymo, Innoviz, WeRide, Karma, Tesla & May Mobility
GOOGL.summary: This article provides an update on recent developments in the autonomous and self-driving vehicle industry, featuring companies like Waymo, Innoviz, WeRide, Karma, Tesla, and May Mobility. Key announcements include Waymo expanding its robotaxi services to Nashville and Germany, Innoviz securing a LiDAR supply deal with Mobileye, and partnerships for right-hand-drive robotaxi deployments. The report also covers legal and safety challenges, such as investigations into Tesla's ADAS role in fatal crashes and a class-action lawsuit against Rivian, alongside insights into consumer trust and market expansion efforts.

The article also covers Alphabet's core businesses, governance model, and where Class C shares are traded.

GOOGL.title: Worth Asset Management LLC Cuts Holdings in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
GOOGL.summary: Worth Asset Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) by 55.7% in the first quarter, selling 4,877 shares and retaining 3,882 shares valued at $1.12 million. Despite this reduction by one investor, institutional ownership of Alphabet remains strong at 40.03%. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock, with an average price target of $413.13, even as the stock recently traded down 1.8% to $337.39 amidst mixed sentiment regarding its AI strategy and advertising revenue.

GOOGL.title: J. L. Bainbridge & Co. Inc. Boosts Holdings in Blackstone Inc. $BX
GOOGL.summary: J. L. Bainbridge & Co. Inc. significantly increased its stake in Blackstone Inc. during the first quarter, purchasing an additional 133,383 shares, bringing its total to 313,105 shares valued at approximately $36 million. Institutional investors maintain a high ownership around 70%, with several other firms also initiating new positions. Despite some analyst target reductions, Blackstone holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating, and has seen notable insider buying activity in recent months.

GOOGL.title: Sterling Capital Management LLC Has $7.30 Million Stock Holdings in United Rentals, Inc. $URI
GOOGL.summary: Sterling Capital Management LLC reduced its stake in United Rentals (NYSE:URI) by 19.9% in the first quarter, now holding 10,018 shares valued at approximately $7.3 million. Despite mixed quarterly results where revenue increased but EPS missed estimates, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $1,070.50. Insider selling was noted, with the CEO and EVP selling shares worth a combined $25.6 million over the last 90 days.

GOOGL.title: The AI Startup Challenging Tesla and Waymo in the Race to Automate Driving
GOOGL.summary: Wayve, an autonomous vehicle startup, is leveraging its AI-powered driving system to compete with industry leaders like Waymo and Tesla. They recently demonstrated their technology on Jeep Grand Cherokees for Stellantis, resulting in a commitment to deploy the technology across America. This highlights Wayve's emergence as a key partner for traditional automakers aiming to integrate self-driving capabilities.
